title: User Persona Canvas
content: Persona template includes: Identity (name/age/role/location/tech-savviness); Background; Goals (primary/secondary); Frustrations; Motivations; Behaviors/Preferences; Scenario; Requirements Implications (Must Have/Should Have/Avoid).

title: Persona Example: Busy Manager Maria
content: Maria Chen age 38 Regional Sales Manager Medium-High tech savviness. Frustrations: too many systems/outdated reports/poor mobile. Must Have: mobile-first dashboard/real-time data/push notifications. Avoid: complex navigation/long forms.

title: Design Thinking 5-Stage Process
content: Five stages: Empathize (observe/interview/immerse) → Define (insights/POV/problem statement) → Ideate (brainstorm/crazy 8s) → Prototype (lo-fi/hi-fi/MVP) → Test (usability/feedback/iterate). BA maps to all stages.
tags: design-thinking empathize define ideate prototype test ux

title: Customer Journey Map - Template Structure
content: Journey Map rows: Stages (Awareness/Consideration/Purchase/Use/Support); Doing (actions); Thinking (thoughts); Feeling (emotions with curve); Touchpoints; Pain Points; Opportunities. Persona and scenario named at top.

title: Journey Map Stage: Awareness
content: Customer actions: search online/ask peers. Thoughts: need a solution/what is available. Opportunities: clear message/SEO. Pain Points: too many options/jargon. Emotion starts neutral and may dip.

title: Journey Map Stage: Consideration
content: Customer compares options/reads reviews/requests demo. Thinks: which is best/can I afford it. Pain: pricing unclear/no free trial. Opportunities: comparison page/free trial.

title: Journey Map Stage: Purchase/Sign-Up
content: Customer fills form/pays/verifies. Thinks: is this secure/what if I change mind. Pain: form too long/payment failed. Opportunities: simple checkout/save progress.

title: Empathy Map Canvas - Four Quadrants
content: Empathy Map quadrants: SAYS (quotes/statements); THINKS (what is on mind/worries); DOES (actions/behaviors); FEELS (emotions/attitudes). Bottom section: PAINS (fears/obstacles) and GAINS (wants/success measures).

title: Empathy Map Says vs Thinks Distinction
content: SAYS captures verbatim quotes like 'I do not have time for this'. THINKS captures unstated concerns like 'Will this work?' or 'I hope I do not look stupid'. Gap between says and thinks reveals hidden needs.

title: Empathy Map Pains vs Gains Analysis
content: PAINS: wasting time on admin/looking incompetent/missing info/making costly mistakes. GAINS: get work done faster/look professional/be informed/make confident decisions. Drive requirement prioritization.

title: Jobs-To-Be-Done (JTBD) Job Story Format
content: JTBD format: WHEN [situation/context/trigger] I WANT TO [motivation/goal] SO I CAN [expected outcome/benefit]. Example: WHEN reviewing sales before meeting I WANT TO identify underperforming regions SO I CAN prepare action plans.

title: JTBD Job Map - 8 Steps
content: Job Map phases: Define → Locate → Prepare → Confirm → Execute → Monitor → Modify → Conclude. Each phase represents a step in completing the core job. Use to identify where system must support user.

title: JTBD Opportunity Scoring Formula
content: Opportunity Score = Importance + (Importance - Satisfaction). Score > 10 = high opportunity (underserved). Score 5-10 = medium. Score < 5 = low (adequately served). Use to prioritize features by unmet needs.

title: Service Blueprint - 4 Layers
content: Service Blueprint layers: Customer Actions (visible); Frontstage Actions (visible staff/system); Backstage Actions (invisible system); Support Processes (infrastructure). Separated by Lines: Interaction/Visibility/Internal Interaction.

title: Usability Metrics ISO 9241-11
content: Three dimensions: Effectiveness (task completion rate/error rate/accuracy); Efficiency (time on task/clicks/learning time); Satisfaction (SUS score >68=good/NPS/CSAT). All three must be specified in usability requirements.

title: Usability Requirement Template
content: Template: UX-ID / Context ([Persona] performing [task] on [device]) / Requirement ([X]% of users SHALL complete [task] within [time/clicks] on first attempt) / Measurement (N participants / success criteria / SUS score).

title: Usability Requirement Example: Registration
content: UX-001: 90% of users SHALL complete registration within 2 minutes with no more than 1 error on first attempt. Measured via usability test with 15 participants. Success: 90% completion/avg <2min/error <10%/SUS>75.

title: UX Requirements Checklist - User Research
content: User research checklist: personas created and validated; customer journey maps completed; empathy maps for key personas; jobs-to-be-done identified; pain points and opportunities documented.

title: UX Requirements Checklist - Documentation
content: Documentation checklist: usability requirements are measurable; task scenarios documented; success criteria defined; accessibility requirements specified (WCAG level); device/browser support matrix; error handling UX defined.

title: UX Requirements Checklist - Validation
content: Validation checklist: personas reviewed with real users; journey maps validated; prototype testing planned; usability testing criteria defined; success metrics agreed upon by stakeholders.

title: UX Requirements Checklist - Handoff
content: Handoff checklist: UX artifacts linked to requirements; design rationale documented; interaction patterns specified; edge cases and error states defined; developer annotations included.

title: UX Elicitation Questions - Understanding Users
content: Key questions: Who are primary users and roles? Technical proficiency level? Context/environment of use? Which devices/browsers must be supported? Any accessibility needs? Language/localization required?
tags: elicitation questions ux users accessibility localization

title: UX Elicitation Questions - Understanding Tasks
content: Questions: Top 5 tasks users need? Frequency of each task? Current process and pain points? Acceptable time to complete? Common errors that occur? These define core task scenarios and success metrics.

title: UX Elicitation Questions - Understanding Context
content: Context questions: Where physically will users be? Will they be interrupted or multi-tasking? Network environment (offline support needed)? Are there time pressures or stress factors? Impacts NFR requirements.
tags: elicitation context offline mobile interruptions stress-factors

title: UX Elicitation Questions - Success Metrics
content: Success questions: How will UX success be measured? What would delightful look like? Competitor products to benchmark against? Biggest usability risk? Defines KPIs and acceptance criteria for UX.

title: Card Sorting Technique
content: Card sorting asks users to group topics/features into categories that make sense to them. Open card sort: users create own groups. Closed card sort: users sort into predefined groups. Reveals mental models for IA.

title: Affinity Diagramming Technique
content: Affinity diagrams organize large amounts of qualitative data into clusters. Steps: write each observation on sticky note; individually sort without talking; group similar notes; name each cluster; identify patterns and insights.

title: User Interview vs Stakeholder Interview
content: User interview: exploratory/behavioral/open-ended/focused on tasks and frustrations. Stakeholder interview: goals/constraints/success metrics/business context. Different objectives require different question sets.

title: Contextual Inquiry Method
content: Contextual Inquiry: observe and interview users in their own environment while they perform real tasks. Four principles: Context (go to user); Partnership (collaborative); Interpretation (discuss meaning); Focus (pre-defined research questions).

title: Think-Aloud Protocol
content: Think-aloud: participants verbalize thoughts while using system. Concurrent think-aloud: during task. Retrospective: after task with recording. Captures cognitive process and reveals hidden usability issues.

title: System Usability Scale (SUS)
content: SUS: 10-item questionnaire scored 0-100. Score interpretation: <51=Poor; 51-68=OK; 68-80=Good; 80-90=Excellent; >90=Best. Alternating positive/negative items. Calculate: sum odd items minus 5; sum 25 minus even items; multiply total by 2.5.

title: Heuristic Evaluation - Nielsen's 10
content: Ten heuristics: 1.Visibility of system status; 2.Match with real world; 3.User control/undo; 4.Consistency/standards; 5.Error prevention; 6.Recognition not recall; 7.Flexibility/efficiency; 8.Aesthetic design; 9.Error recovery; 10.Help/documentation.

title: Usability Testing Script Template
content: Script sections: Introduction and consent; Warm-up questions about background; Task scenarios with realistic context; Post-task questions (difficulty rating/what confused you); Post-test SUS questionnaire; Debrief and open questions.

title: WCAG 2.1 Accessibility - 4 Principles (POUR)
content: POUR principles: Perceivable (info available to all senses); Operable (UI usable by all input methods); Understandable (content/operation clear); Robust (compatible with current/future technologies). AA level required for most systems.
tags: wcag accessibility a11y pour compliance requirements

title: WCAG AA Accessibility Checklist
content: WCAG AA checks: alt text for images; captions for video; color contrast ratio 4.5:1 minimum; keyboard navigable; focus visible; no seizure-inducing content; error identification; labels for all form inputs; consistent navigation.

title: Accessibility Requirements Pattern
content: Pattern: The system SHALL [accessibility criterion] so that users with [disability type] can [accomplish task]. Example: The system SHALL provide text alternatives for all non-text content so that screen reader users can access all information.

title: Task Analysis Method
content: Task analysis breaks complex tasks into subtasks/steps. Hierarchical Task Analysis (HTA): goal → subtasks → operations. Cognitive Task Analysis (CTA): captures knowledge/decisions. Output feeds into use cases and usability testing scenarios.

title: Information Architecture Patterns
content: IA patterns: Hierarchical (tree structure/categories); Sequential (step-by-step wizard); Matrix (user chooses navigation path); Hub and spoke (central hub/no cross-linking). Select based on content volume and user mental models.

title: User Research Synthesis - Key Steps
content: Synthesis steps: collect all raw data; transcribe and tag observations; identify patterns using affinity diagrams; prioritize insights by frequency and impact; translate insights into requirements and design principles.

title: Design Thinking BA Activities Mapping
content: BA maps to DT stages: Empathize (user research/stakeholder interviews); Define (requirements synthesis/problem framing); Ideate (solution exploration with stakeholders); Prototype (validate requirements with mockups); Test (UAT/usability validation).

title: Persona Validation Checklist
content: Validate personas: based on real research data (not assumptions); reviewed by actual users or proxies; covers all primary user segments; contains specific behavioral patterns (not demographics only); has actionable requirements implications.

title: UX Research Plan Template
content: UX research plan: Research questions (what do we need to learn?); Methods (which techniques?); Participants (who/how many/screening criteria?); Timeline; Deliverables (personas/journey maps/insights report); Resources needed.

title: Requirement from Journey Map Pain Point
content: Pattern: identify pain point in journey map → write problem statement → derive requirement. Example: Pain = payment fails with no feedback → Problem = users do not know what to fix → Requirement = system SHALL display specific error message with resolution steps.

title: Mobile UX Requirements Considerations
content: Mobile-specific UX requirements: touch targets minimum 44x44px; thumb-zone friendly layout; offline capability specification; network degradation graceful handling; push notification requirements; one-handed operation support.

title: UX Skill Dependencies and Outputs
content: SKILL-14 dependencies: SKILL-01 (core BA) and SKILL-02 (elicitation). Outputs: Personas; Journey Maps; Empathy Maps; UX Requirements. Related: SKILL-02 (interviews); SKILL-03 (writing requirements); SKILL-15 (workshops).
